commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an intentional-disconnect flag to the Discord GatewayPlugin close path so abnormal closes can re-enable reconnect after `shouldReconnect` was cleared, with two regression tests.

PR surface: Source +10, Tests +50. Total +60 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence real reproduction is included. Source inspection shows current main would suppress reconnect if an active socket closes abnormally while `shouldReconnect` is false, but the PR demonstrates that state by forcing private fields.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer action is needed to add real behavior proof and verify the reachable stale-state path; there is no narrow ClawSweeper code repair to queue yet.

target.title: fix(discord): reconnect after abnormal gateway close
target.body:
## Summary
- re-arm Discord gateway reconnect after abnormal non-intentional closes when shouldReconnect had been cleared
- preserve intentional disconnect and normal close behavior
- add regression coverage for abnormal 1006 recovery and intentional disconnect suppression

## Verification
- pnpm exec vitest run extensions/discord/src/internal/gateway.test.ts --config vitest.config.ts
- PATH=/usr/local/bin:/usr/local/opt/node/bin:/usr/bin:/bin /usr/local/bin/openclaw gateway call status --json --timeout 5000
- confirmed live launchd target uses /usr/local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/index.js and patched runtime bundle /usr/local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/provider-D1OlwjUp.js
- imported the installed /usr/local provider bundle and forced stale shouldReconnect=false + close 1006 with a fake WebSocket; result: shouldReconnect=true, reconnectAttempts=1, scheduledDelay=2000, errors=[]

Paperclip: SUP-2240
target.author: levineam
